ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station (e.g., New York, NY, USA) */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the weather station */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Geographical latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Geographical longitude of the weather station */,
  "altitude" TEXT /* Elevation of the weather station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the weather station was installed */,
  "maintenance_schedule" TEXT /* Scheduled maintenance dates for the weather station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather data record */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the station where the data was collected */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time of data collection */,
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Type of weather data (e.g., temperature, humidity) */,
  "collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the data (e.g., manual, automated) */,
  "collection_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of data collection (e.g., hourly, daily) */,
  "quality_control" TEXT /* Quality control checks performed on the data */,
  "windspeed" REAL /* Wind speed in meters per second */,
  "humidity" INTEGER /* Relative humidity in percentage */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "pressure" INTEGER /* Atmospheric pressure in hPa */,
  "rain" REAL /* Rainfall in millimeters */,
  "snow" REAL /* Snowfall in millimeters */,
  "clouds" INTEGER /* Cloud cover percentage */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the weather (e.g., rain, clouds) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the data type (e.g., temperature, humidity) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data type */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the data type (e.g., Celsius, Fahrenheit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data file */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station the file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the data file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the data file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the data file (e.g., CSV, JSON) */,
  "file_compression" TEXT /* Compression algorithm used for the data file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_files_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "readme_files" (
  "readme_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each readme file */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station the readme file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the readme file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the readme file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "readme_format" TEXT /* Format of the readme file (e.g., TXT, PDF) */,
  "readme_contents" TEXT /* Contents of the readme file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("readme_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readme_files_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the user's role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method used to access the data (e.g., API, web interface) */,
  "access_ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version of the data */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the changes in this version */,
  "version_author" TEXT /* User who created the version */,
  "version_comments" TEXT /* Comments about the changes made in the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high temperature, heavy rain) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date the alert was generated */,
  "alert_threshold" REAL /* Threshold value that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the alert (e.g., high, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id")
)
